faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Secaucus home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere clothing, visible larvae or cocoons in closets, flying moths near wardrobes, and damaged carpets or upholstery. You may also notice a musty odor or see webbing in fabric folds. Our technicians provide thorough inspections to confirm the extent of any infestation.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control treatment involve?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive treatment includes a detailed inspection of closets, storage areas, and affected fabrics, followed by targeted treatments using safe insecticides and pheromone traps. We also provide guidance on cleaning infested items, proper storage techniques, and prevention strategies to protect your textiles long-term.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth control cost between $550 and $5,000?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of the affected area, the number of rooms or closets requiring treatment, and whether follow-up visits are needed. Smaller, localized infestations typically cost $550-$1,200, while extensive infestations in larger homes or commercial properties requiring multiple treatments can reach $5,000.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing seasonal clothing in sealed containers, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural repellents, and maintaining low humidity levels. We recommend professional inspections annually and immediately dry-cleaning or freezing any newly purchased vintage or second-hand clothing before storing it with your wardrobe.
